name: prettier-catspeak
description: A code formatter for Catspeak built with Prettier

prettier.catspeak.commaMode.description: Specifies how commas should be printed.
markdownEnumDescriptions: Print no commas except when required. `fn(a, [b] c)`
Print commas in a 'normal' manner. `fn(a, [b], c)`
Print trailing commas when the statement wraps to a new line. `fn(a, [b, ], c, )`

prettier.catspeak.doubleIndent.description: Enable/disable double indenting some wrapped expression blocks, such as if conditions, accessors, and function calls.

prettier.catspeak.printSemicolons.description: If enabled, each statement will be terminated with a semicolon.

prettier.catspeak.emptyFunctionArguments.markdownDescription: If enabled, function declarations with no arguments will not have parenthesis. `let fn = fun { ... }`

prettier.catspeak.wrapBinaryOperators.description: If enabled, wrapped binary operation expressions will place their operator on a newline before the second operand.

prettier.catspeak.parseCatchThrow.description: If enabled, catch and throw expressions will be parsed and both keywords will be forbidden as identifiers.
